text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris Pentecost Sunday May 24 2026 This weekend May 23rd 24th the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Pentecost Marking fifty days after the Resurrection this is the day that the Church was first strengthened by the Holy Spirit to proclaim the gospel message boldly to the world This event is recorded in the second chapter of the Acts of the Apostles explaining how the Apostles are now empowered to go forth and make disciples of all nations Matthew 2819 The Holy Spirit that descended upon the Apostles at Pentecost continues to enliven the Church even today The Lord continues to send out His Spirit to renew the face of the earth and to draw all of us and all of fallen creation back to Himself And just as the descent of the Spirit empowered the Apostles to preach the good news so are we likewise empowered by the same Spirit We must trust the Holy Spirit to enable us to carry out this responsibility As Pope St Paul VI witnessed the Holy Spirit is the principal agent of evangelization On Evangelization Paragraph 75 The Holy Spirit will help us to proclaim the Gospel by word and by deed to those whom our lives touch The Holy Spirit will work in those to whom we witness that Christ's truth may be understood and accepted In that confidence let us all be prepared to go forth to proclaim the gospel Memorial Day Mass You are invited to join us this Memorial Day Monday May 25th at 10 AM for a special Mass at St Joseph Catholic Cemetery on SR 124 as we pray for all those who have died in service to our country and for all the faithful departed In the midst of remembrance and gratitude we commend those who have given their lives for our freedoms to the mercy of God and place our hope in the Resurrection of Jesus Christ Corpus Christi Procession On Sunday June 7th the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Corpus Christi Following our 10 AM Mass on Sunday June 7th we will hold our annual Corpus Christi Procession In every Eucharistic Procession we follow after the Lord physically as we pledge ourselves to do so spiritually We also give public witness to our acceptance of the Lord's words The bread that I will give is my flesh for the life of the world John 651 Graduation Mass On Sunday June 14th at the 10 AM Mass we will celebrate our annual Graduate's Mass for our graduating High School Seniors If you have a high school senior who is graduating and would like to be honored at this Mass please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information General Update Parish Renovations This weekend was originally scheduled to mark the end of our renovation project Materials delays however have pushed our end date by at least a month We are expecting the remaining flooring to arrive and be installed by the end of the June We are still experiencing delays with the woodworking package being milled by Big Lumber out of Fort Wayne As of this past Friday we were assured that our project is next in line This means that we will hopefully see parts coming in for installation starting this week Open Position Part time Parish Custodian In anticipation for our completed renovations we will be adding a second custodial position to our Parish Staff to assist with the increased cleaning responsibilities This hourly position would focus on maintaining the Schaefer Parish Center which includes both the Religious Education Classrooms and Gymnasium Starting at 14 per hour with an increase to 15 after four months this position offers flexible working hours Please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information Saturday May 23 400 4:45pm Confession
